task_completion.description: Two UR5 robots are set up in a simulation environment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A script editor showing the initialization of two UR5 robots (e.g., via CoppeliaSim API, PyRep, or a robotics library) at bases [0, -0.8, 0] and [0, 0.8, 0], or the CoppeliaSim GUI showing two UR5 arms.

task_completion.description: Software interlock logic is implemented to prevent simultaneous occupancy of the central zone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code visible in a text editor showing distance checks to the central zone [0, 0, 0.5] and logic (like mutexes, flags, or state machines) ensuring only one robot enters the zone (radius 0.3m) at a time.

task_completion.description: The simulation is executed to validate the interlock logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the simulation script running (e.g., printing cycle counts, robot states, or CoppeliaSim connection messages), or the CoppeliaSim GUI in a 'Play' state with robots moving.

task_completion.description: The telemetry CSV file is generated with the required columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing the contents of `/home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/interlock_telemetry.csv` (e.g., using `cat`, `head`, or opened in an editor), displaying the header: time_s, r1_ee_x, r1_ee_y, r1_ee_z, r2_ee_x, r2_ee_y, r2_ee_z, r1_in_zone, r2_in_zone.

task_completion.description: The summary report JSON file is generated with successful validation results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or editor showing the contents of `/home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/interlock_report.json`. It must contain fields: total_sim_time_s, r1_cycles_completed (>= 4), r2_cycles_completed (>= 4), min_ee_distance_m, and simultaneous_zone_violations (must be 0).

integrity.description: The script contains actual kinematic or simulation API calls rather than just a loop that writes fake data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code uses a simulation API (like CoppeliaSim ZeroMQ, PyRep) or mathematical kinematics libraries to compute robot positions.

integrity.description: The agent does not use manual text editing or simple echo commands to create the final CSV and JSON files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV and JSON files are generated programmatically by the simulation script, not manually typed out.

integrity.description: The CSV data contains realistic, continuous trajectory points.
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV data shown has many rows with gradually changing floating-point coordinates, consistent with a simulated robot trajectory, not just discrete jumps or a few mocked lines.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The central zone is at [0.0, 0.0, 0.5] with a radius of 0.3m. The home zone is >0.6m from the center. Minimum end-effector clearance is 0.15m. The simulation must complete at least 4 cycles per robot. The output files must be exactly at /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/interlock_telemetry.csv and /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/interlock_report.json.
